explanation: This formula specifies that whenever prop_1 happens, prop_0 must occur at the same time. In other words, at every moment where prop_1 is true, prop_0 should also be true.
This expresses that whenever 'prop_1' holds, 'prop_0' must also hold at the same position in time. That is, 'prop_0' must occur whenever 'prop_1' occurs.
This means that whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must also be true at the same position. In LTL, for every point in time, if prop_1 holds, then prop_0 holds.
This means that whenever prop_1 holds, prop_0 also holds at the same moment.
This expresses that whenever prop_1 holds, prop_0 must also hold at the same moment. In other words, at every point in time, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 is also true.

This means that whenever prop_0 occurs, it occurs at a point where prop_1 also holds, and prop_1 does not hold in the next moment. In other words, prop_0 can only be true on the final occurrence of prop_1.
This requires that whenever prop_1 holds, then prop_0 must also hold at that time. In LTL, this is expressed globally: whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 is also true.
The statement means that whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must also be true at the same time. This is captured in LTL as globally (prop_1 implies prop_0).
This sentence means that whenever prop_1 occurs, then prop_0 must also hold at that very same time. In LTL, this can be expressed as: globally, if prop_1 holds, then prop_0 also holds.

The statement requires that whenever prop_1 holds, then prop_0 must also hold at that same moment, i.e., whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must be true as well. This is expressed as globally (prop_1 -> prop_0).
